title: How do You Respond to People Saying Eva is Pretentious
display_title: How do You Respond to People Saying Eva is Pretentious
wikitext:
[]: How do You Respond to People Saying Eva is Pretentious?             Quote Post by EVAfacepalm » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:02 am This is just something I thought I'd ask after looking through comments on Eva-related stuff. What's your personal counter-argument (if any, ignoring them is valid too) to claims that the show is "pretentious", "emo", or, "overrated"?

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:04 am Shrug it off and listen to Muppets rap about jackhammers.

[]: Re: How do You Respond to People Saying Eva is Pretentious?             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:21 am I tell them there is psychological drama in it. The characters are quite complex if you pay attention to it. Emo is just a jaded way to mock drama involving teenagers. As for overrated. Its bound to happen to anything. However I do agree with the usage of religion crosses. They do come off as silly. The fact that the directors say its there to look cool doesn't help it. It does ruin a bit of the soundtrack with Asuka getting mind raped and probably ode to joy. Ditto for the budget stuff(except for Kaworu's, I thought it was handled well). I think that for religious subtext, its not obvious stuff. I remember I saw a thread that found quite a few in the comatose scene even involving the scenes right after that with Bridge Bunnies. One other stuff people seem to use to call it pretentious are some screenshots of an interview he did where school children was involved and he said some odd things ("I used that name cause it sounds complicated"). I think he was just joking around and/or didn't want to say the entire thing at the time. Plenty of stuff in media that have different ways of normal story telling have this. MGS2 is another example that I see often with similar complaints.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:24 am Minute 0:42

[]:             Quote Post by CX1329 » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:31 am It depends. If they haven't watched it, then they're clearly not qualified to express an informed opinion. If they have, then I guess that's how they feel about the anime and it can't really be helped, seeing as those adjectives are all subjective and different people will have different preferences.

[]:             Quote Post by Katana » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:35 am 2 DEEP 4 U

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:51 am I explain to them how Evangelion is actually an alchemical formula to destroy the universe. Most don't understand. They're the lucky ones.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Jun 03, 2013 1:06 am I ask them why they think so. Since it's a work of art, it's very often the case that the person calling it pretentious simply doesn't enjoy the style. If so, that's too bad. All I can do in this case is say why I personally like the series and hope they understand that our views are both subjective. Of course, the word "pretentious" is thrown around so much nowadays that anyone who uses it as a blanket term is rather likely to not be someone to give much actual consideration to anything.

[]: Re: How do You Respond to People Saying Eva is Pretentious?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jun 03, 2013 2:45 am EVAfacepalm wrote: What's your personal counter-argument to claims that the show is "pretentious", "emo", or, "overrated"?

[]: Re: How do You Respond to People Saying Eva is Pretentious?             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 03, 2013 2:59 am The Eva Monkey wrote: But it is pretentious, emo, and overrated. It's also really good despite all of that.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Jun 03, 2013 3:06 am Just say: "Well, it's a pretentious show for pretentious people.", and walk away.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Mon Jun 03, 2013 3:06 am I say that it is, and it's fantastic how a simple insight into the lives of the miserable could be so pretentious. Then I laugh.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Jun 03, 2013 3:19 am SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jun 03, 2013 4:09 am ...I don't. Let them think what they want, ain't doin' no harm to me or my own.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Jun 03, 2013 5:14 am Katana wrote: 2 DEEP 4 U

[]:             Quote Post by Katana » Mon Jun 03, 2013 5:18 am Fireball wrote: This is the only appropriate answer.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 03, 2013 5:28 am To be pretentious requires an aspect of pretence - I would ask them where that was apparent.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Jun 03, 2013 6:04 am Ask why he is projecting his own fear of being too pretentious on a chinese cartoon

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jun 03, 2013 6:15 am You tell'em, Vegeta.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jun 03, 2013 6:21 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: Ask why he is projecting his own fear of being too pretentious on a chinese cartoon
